items.name: Thunderbird Appointment
items.source_story_title: Cal.com is going closed source
items.summary: Open-source scheduling tool positioned as a Cal.com replacement.
items.why_included: A Thunderbird maintainer explicitly pitched it as the open-source replacement path.
items.evidence: “Our scheduling tool, Thunderbird Appointment, will always be open source” and “We’ll help you replace Cal.com”.

items.name: schedyou
items.source_story_title: Cal.com is going closed source
items.summary: Small self-hosted scheduling tool built to replace a Cal.com instance.
items.why_included: A commenter described it as a simpler self-hosted Cal.com replacement they built themselves.
items.evidence: “replace my Cal.com instance with a smaller, simpler self-hosted tool”.

items.name: cal.diy
items.source_story_title: Cal.com is going closed source
items.summary: The Cal.com fork commenters pointed to as the obvious alternative.
items.why_included: It was directly recommended as the thing people who care should use instead.
items.evidence: “anyone who cares would just use cal.diy as they were prior to this announcement”.

items.name: PiCore
items.source_story_title: PiCore - Raspberry Pi Port of Tiny Core Linux
items.summary: An immutable Tiny Core Linux port for Raspberry Pi hardware.
items.why_included: Commenters praised the distro itself as a clever immutable Linux build.
items.evidence: “A very clever immutable Linux distro”.

items.name: PiCorePlayer
items.source_story_title: PiCore - Raspberry Pi Port of Tiny Core Linux
items.summary: A Pi-based Squeezebox/Lyrion client/server setup that commenters love.
items.why_included: It was explicitly called a favorite and a practical use case for PiCore.
items.evidence: “the basis for the excellent PiCorePlayer, a favourite of mine to run Squeezebox clients (and/or Lyrion music server) on any Pi”.

items.name: SmartTube
items.source_story_title: YouTube now lets you turn off Shorts
items.summary: An Android/TV YouTube client people use specifically to avoid Shorts.
items.why_included: A commenter said it was the reason they switched away from YouTube on Chromecast.
items.evidence: “#1 reason I switched to the SmartTube app on my Chromecast. No more Shorts.”

items.name: SponsorBlock
items.source_story_title: YouTube now lets you turn off Shorts
items.summary: Crowd-sourced sponsor skipping for YouTube.
items.why_included: It was recommended as a clean way to skip sponsor segments.
items.evidence: “SponsorBlock - Crowd-sourced skip sponsored segments”.

items.name: DeArrow
items.source_story_title: YouTube now lets you turn off Shorts
items.summary: A tool for making YouTube thumbnails less clickbait-y.
items.why_included: Commenters recommended it for reducing clickbait on YouTube.
items.evidence: “DeArrow - Make thumbnails not clickbait”.

items.name: Unhook
items.source_story_title: YouTube now lets you turn off Shorts
items.summary: A Firefox extension for hiding Shorts and tailoring YouTube’s UI.
items.why_included: It was directly recommended as a YouTube customization layer.
items.evidence: “I use the ‘Unhook’ Firefox extension to customize the YouTube page. You can hide shorts…”.

items.name: ublock-hide-yt-shorts
items.source_story_title: YouTube now lets you turn off Shorts
items.summary: A uBlock Origin filter repo for hiding YouTube Shorts.
items.why_included: The thread pointed to it as a practical filter-based workaround.
items.evidence: “Just use uBlock origin and this filter: https://github.com/i5heu/ublock-hide-yt-shorts”.

items.name: Invidious
items.source_story_title: YouTube now lets you turn off Shorts
items.summary: A privacy-friendly YouTube frontend used as an alternative to the main site.
items.why_included: A commenter said they use it to watch YouTube videos after dropping Google services.
items.evidence: “I deleted my Google account and now occasionally use Invidious with LibRedirect”.

items.name: LibRedirect
items.source_story_title: YouTube now lets you turn off Shorts
items.summary: A browser extension that redirects services to privacy-friendly frontends.
items.why_included: It was part of a concrete YouTube-alternative setup.
items.evidence: “use Invidious with LibRedirect to watch YT videos”.

items.name: OpenClaw
items.source_story_title: Ask HN: Who is using OpenClaw?
items.summary: An AI tool with active users saying it remains helpful and compelling.
items.why_included: Multiple commenters reported real use and positive experience with it.
items.evidence: Users said they “still use it and find it helpful” and that it is “compelling”.

items.name: Hermes Agent
items.source_story_title: Ask HN: Who is using OpenClaw?
items.summary: An AI agent platform praised for being easier to keep working than predecessors.
items.why_included: A commenter said it was the first one that did not feel like constant repair work.
items.evidence: “it didn’t feel like I was constantly needing to fix it, so at the moment I’m happy with it”.

items.name: Claude Cowork for PowerPoint
items.source_story_title: ChatGPT for Excel
items.summary: A deck-generation tool praised for both content and design quality.
items.why_included: A commenter was genuinely surprised by how strong the slides looked.
items.evidence: “I was stunned by the content as well as design quality of the deck it produced”.

items.name: Claude for Excel
items.source_story_title: ChatGPT for Excel
items.summary: A spreadsheet assistant commenters say is already very capable.
items.why_included: It was described as good enough for real junior-level spreadsheet work.
items.evidence: “Claude for excel is already amazing. Fully capable of doing junior work.”

items.name: ChatGPT for Excel
items.source_story_title: ChatGPT for Excel
items.summary: A spreadsheet-oriented ChatGPT experience that commenters want to try and like on complex sheets.
items.why_included: The thread contained positive anticipation and concrete praise of its spreadsheet handling.
items.evidence: One commenter said they were looking forward to trying it, and another said it “does really well on complex sheets.”

items.name: Gemini app
items.source_story_title: The Gemini app is now on Mac
items.summary: Google’s Gemini app on macOS drew positive comparisons to Claude.
items.why_included: A commenter said the launch was enough to make them try Gemini over Claude.
items.evidence: “This is enough to make me try Gemini over Claude.”

items.name: Dragon Book
items.source_story_title: Want to write a compiler? Just read these two papers (2008)
items.summary: Classic compiler text recommended as a good introductory book.
items.why_included: Commenters praised it as a self-sufficient introduction and a good place to start.
items.evidence: “Chapter 2 is a self-sufficient introduction into compilers” and “I found it to be a good introductory text.”

items.name: Compilers by Niklaus Wirth
items.source_story_title: Want to write a compiler? Just read these two papers (2008)
items.summary: A short compiler book praised for clarity and completeness.
items.why_included: A commenter called it a fantastic intro with pristine clarity.
items.evidence: “Another fantastic intro to compiler writing is the short little book ‘Compilers’ by Niklaus Wirth” with “pristine clarity”.
